certified_mail.invite: person whose affairs have caught the attention of something with legal standing
certified_mail.desc: USPS Certified Mail ($4.35 + postage) requires a signature. That signature is the mechanic — the participant must actively receive it; it becomes an event, not a passive delivery.

informed_delivery_gap.invite: person being managed by something that works inside bureaucratic infrastructure
informed_delivery_gap.desc: USPS Informed Delivery sends participants a morning email with grayscale scans of incoming mail.

notarized_doc.invite: person with legal standing in something larger than themselves
notarized_doc.desc: Notarization verifies the signing event, not the content or the organization. The embossed seal and wet signature are completely real and legally valid.

llc_registration.invite: person dealing with an organization that has real public standing
llc_registration.desc: Wyoming (~$100/year) or Delaware (~$90/year). The fictional organization has a real state registration number, articles of incorporation, a registered agent address.

mailbox_address.invite: investigator finding the edges of a real-seeming organizational structure
mailbox_address.desc: Regus virtual office (~$49–89/month) gives a "suite" address in a real building. UPS Store PMB ($20–40/month) is cheaper but the "PMB" designation is recognizable.

wax_seal.invite: recipient of correspondence from something with gravitas — a tradition, a society, an institution with ceremony
wax_seal.desc: A wax-sealed envelope is categorically different before it's opened. Nobody receives one in ordinary life. The anachronism signals: ordinary rules don't apply here.

classified_doc_aesthetic.invite: person cleared to see what others aren't — an insider by selection
classified_doc_aesthetic.desc: The visual language of classified and official documents carries weight far beyond its informational content. Key elements:

osint_personalization.invite: person who has been seen and studied before they knew it
osint_personalization.desc: Sources: LinkedIn, Twitter/X, Instagram (public), Strava/fitness (if public), Yelp reviews they've written, Nextdoor, Google Maps reviews, voter registration, property records, loc

local_area_code.invite: person receiving a call from their own world — something that knows where they are
local_area_code.desc: Buy via Twilio with area code matching participant's city. Assign one number per fictional character for consistency.

voicemail_drop.invite: person whose life is being quietly tracked by something patient
voicemail_drop.desc: Services: Slybroadcast, Drop Cowboy, Straight To Voicemail. Deposits voicemail without ringing.

micro_transaction.invite: person whose actual financial life has been touched by the narrative
micro_transaction.desc: Money crossing from fiction into the participant's real financial history is the strongest reality-threshold play in the library. The note field is the narrative payload.

matched_prefix.invite: person being called by something that knows their specific world — not just their city, their neighborhood
matched_prefix.desc: Same area code AND same 3-digit prefix as participant's own number. Twilio number search supports pattern matching.

implied_prior_relationship.invite: person re-entering a relationship they don't remember making — someone with a history they're only now discovering
implied_prior_relationship.desc: An email or message framed as a continuation of something that hasn't happened yet.

planted_object.invite: person whose physical world has been entered — not just their inbox
planted_object.desc: An unexplained object appears in the participant's personal space. Not at a designated pickup location — in their home, on their car, at their desk.

accidental_transmission.invite: person who accidentally found themselves behind a curtain
accidental_transmission.desc: The most effective pure-email technique. A message that appears to have been sent to the participant by mistake shifts them from passive receiver to active discoverer.

real_domain.invite: investigator finding a real trail — not just a message, but an organization with infrastructure
real_domain.desc: Domain (~$15/year) + hosting (Netlify/Vercel free tiers work for static sites) + Let's Encrypt SSL (free). The organization holds up to Googling.

classified_ad.invite: person who found what the ad was placed for them to find — the intended recipient
classified_ad.desc: Something findable but not pushed. The act of finding it is the payload. Digital classifieds get indexed; print creates a permanent physical artifact.

physical_dead_drop.invite: operative retrieving something left specifically for them by someone who knew they'd come
physical_dead_drop.desc: An object at a location they actually go implies prior surveillance of their movements. The experience has been in their physical world before they knew about it.

qr_code.invite: person who follows threads on their own initiative — an active participant rather than a passive recipient
qr_code.desc: URL behind the code can serve time-gated content: before the arc beat, a single cryptic line or holding image; after, the full revelation. Same physical code, living digital layer.

annotation_layer.invite: person who found what others reading the same document missed
annotation_layer.desc: Hypothesis (hypothes.is) is a web annotation platform that allows public annotations on any webpage.

linkedin_character.invite: person interacting with someone who has verifiable professional standing in the real world
linkedin_character.desc: Build at least 4 weeks before the experience. Key elements: posting history (irregular timing, mix of professional and personal), 50+ connections including mutual connections with

google_maps.invite: investigator confirming a real physical location for the organization
google_maps.desc: A pin on the map. Participant who looks up the address finds a real business listing with photos, hours, description, and potentially reviews.

press_release.invite: person dealing with something that has had real media coverage
press_release.desc: EIN Presswire ($50–100) indexes in Google News within 24–48 hours. The fictional organization's name now returns real news results.

aged_social_media.invite: investigator finding the character's social history — a public record of a person who has existed
aged_social_media.desc: Twitter/X or Instagram. Post at irregular, human-seeming intervals. Content ratio: ~80% mundane professional/personal, ~20% narrative-adjacent.

ai_generated_face.invite: investigator trying to place a face that doesn't quite connect to anyone they know
ai_generated_face.desc: Generate with fixed seed parameter. Save the seed + full prompt — consistency requires identical parameters.

reddit_history.invite: researcher who found the character's candid voice — not the official one
reddit_history.desc: A fictional character's Reddit comment history is a window into their inner life that LinkedIn and Instagram don't provide.

github_repo.invite: investigator finding the technical footprint of something real and active
github_repo.desc: A GitHub account with a real commit history creates a character who works in code. The repository itself can contain:

goodreads_profile.invite: person catching a glimpse of a character's intellectual and emotional interior
goodreads_profile.desc: A character's Goodreads or Letterboxd profile is among the most intimate self-portrait available online — what someone reads or watches reveals how they think and what they care ab

weather_message.invite: person whose immediate physical environment is being watched — not just their digital behavior
weather_message.desc: OpenWeatherMap free tier. Query at send time and insert one accurate real-time detail. One specific detail does disproportionate work.

calendar_invite.invite: person whose schedule is known and is being scheduled against by something
calendar_invite.desc: Send via SMTP with iCal attachment — works with Google, Outlook, Apple Calendar. The fictional character appears in the participant's actual calendar.

fedex_tracking.invite: recipient of a real delivery from a real organization with real logistics
fedex_tracking.desc: Real tracking number given before arrival turns the logistics system into narrative pacing.

past_tense_timestamp.invite: person who has been observed for longer than they knew
past_tense_timestamp.desc: A message written as though observation began before this moment.

timed_delivery.invite: person whose daily life has been studied in enough detail to know when they're most likely to be reached
timed_delivery.desc: Something arriving right before or right after a known real event reads as fated.

restaurant_reservation.invite: guest of an organization that can make things happen in the physical world
restaurant_reservation.desc: A real reservation, verifiable by calling the restaurant. Reserve under a name or framing meaningful in the narrative.

briefed_confederate.invite: person whose actual life is intersecting with the narrative — not just a recipient of messages
briefed_confederate.desc: - Sponsor — knows the participant best; most motivated; highest risk of emotional bleed for both - Regular hospitality staff (barista, server, hotel front desk) — trained in discre

real_ticket.invite: recipient of a curated invitation — person who has been chosen for an experience someone thought they needed
real_ticket.desc: Two real tickets to a real event. The designed experience ends; the real experience it was pointing toward begins.

gift_card.invite: person valued enough to receive a real gift — the relationship is real enough that something was given
gift_card.desc: Physical Visa/Mastercard prepaid card mailed in a well-designed envelope. The organization has real resources. Face value: $25–75.

library_hold.invite: person whose interests and inner life are being tracked and curated by something
library_hold.desc: Place a real hold on a book at their local library branch using their library card info (with sponsor's help and participant's broad consent).

false_ally.invite: person who found an ally (pre-reveal); person who was fooled by their own judgment (post-reveal) — both identities are productive
false_ally.desc: An operative presents as a fellow participant or accidental co-conspirator — someone else caught in the same game, or someone who stumbled into the participant's situation from the

ai_phone_agent.invite: person receiving a call from an organization with enough resources to reach out by phone
ai_phone_agent.desc: Platforms: Vapi, Bland.ai, Retell AI. The agent knows the participant profile and the character's personality and knowledge state.

voice_clone.invite: person receiving a message from someone they know — or believed they did
voice_clone.desc: ElevenLabs voice clone from 2–5 minutes of clean speech. The voice of someone the participant knows and trusts — a parent, old friend, mentor — saying something that only makes sen

ringless_voicemail.invite: person being reached out to quietly — contacted without alarm, without intrusion

video_message.invite: person being addressed directly and personally by someone who took the time to speak to them specifically
video_message.desc: HeyGen or Synthesia. 30–45 seconds maximum. Slightly imperfect quality — mild compression, not studio lighting.

deepfake.invite: person who witnessed something that couldn't have happened and yet did
deepfake.desc: Someone the participant knows appears to be delivering a message within the narrative. The maximum possible reality-blurring in the library.

aged_photograph.invite: person who has a history they didn't know about — a past that was documented before they arrived
aged_photograph.desc: A physical photograph printed on actual photographic paper (Walgreens, CVS, Snapfish — any drugstore photo print) is categorically different from a home inkjet printout.

polaroid.invite: recipient of something someone kept — an object that implies a prior relationship with whoever took it
polaroid.desc: Polaroid cameras (Polaroid Now, OneStep) and Fujifilm Instax cameras are both available and cheap to use. Instax Mini film is about $1/shot.

torn_journal_page.invite: person who accidentally found someone's private thoughts
torn_journal_page.desc: A page torn from a journal — handwritten, aged, with visible wear — implies a private document that found its way to the participant by a route they don't know.

newspaper_clipping.invite: person who found documentary evidence that something actually happened
newspaper_clipping.desc: A physical newspaper clipping — cut or torn from a real or fake newspaper page.

redacted_document.invite: investigator who found real documentation — but can't see everything yet
redacted_document.desc: Heavy black redaction marks are more interesting than revealed text. What's hidden is always more compelling than what's shown.

handwritten_letter.invite: person valued enough to receive correspondence by hand — someone who warranted that effort
handwritten_letter.desc: A handwritten letter in 2026 is the rarest form of personal communication. Receiving one creates immediate priority attention.

altered_book.invite: person who found something hidden inside a real object — insider access via a physical artifact
altered_book.desc: A physical book that appears ordinary but has been altered — containing a layer of narrative content added by the fictional character.

wrong_person_contact.invite: accidental witness to someone else's story — or the person who was always the intended recipient
wrong_person_contact.desc: A message that's clearly intended for someone with a similar name, or sent to the participant's address for a previous tenant, or addressed to a version of their name they don't us

misdelivered_package.invite: person who received something not meant for them — or who was always the intended recipient
misdelivered_package.desc: Ship a package to the participant's address, addressed to a name that isn't theirs but is meaningful in the narrative.

unsolicited_subscription.invite: person whose identity is being used by something they don't understand yet
unsolicited_subscription.desc: A magazine, newsletter, or product sample arrives in their mail for a subscription they didn't initiate.

returned_letter.invite: recipient of correspondence meant for someone who is no longer where they were — or never was
returned_letter.desc: A letter arrives at the participant's address stamped "RETURN TO SENDER — ADDRESSEE UNKNOWN" or "MOVED — NO FORWARDING ADDRESS." It's addressed to someone who used to live there, o

vanishing_institution.invite: person who can see what others can't — something was real and is now gone, and only they were there for both states
vanishing_institution.desc: At the right arc moment, the organization disappears: the website returns 404, the phone number is disconnected, the LinkedIn profiles are deactivated, the Google Maps listing is r

loyalty_program.invite: member of an organization that values ongoing relationship with them specifically
loyalty_program.desc: A "welcome to your loyalty program" email from the fictional organization. They have a member number. They've accumulated 3,247 points.

missed_appointment.invite: person with existing obligations they've forgotten — someone with a history they didn't know they were building
missed_appointment.desc: A "reminder" or "follow-up" for an appointment that was never made. "[Organization] Appointment Reminder: Your scheduled consultation on [recent past date] was not completed.

receipt_unknown_purchase.invite: person whose financial identity has been touched by something outside their knowledge
receipt_unknown_purchase.desc: A confirmation email styled exactly like a real retailer receipt — for a purchase made "on their account" for items that are meaningful in the narrative.

automated_system_call.invite: account holder receiving notification from a system that knows their account exists
automated_system_call.desc: A call that sounds like an automated system — IVR audio quality, slight compression, the specific cadence of automated messaging.

you_ve_been_found.invite: person who was hunting and discovers they were the one being observed all along
you_ve_been_found.desc: The most powerful structural moment available. Let the participant discover things first — the website, the organization, the character — without being acknowledged.

retroactive_meaning.invite: person at the center of a pattern that was designed for them specifically
retroactive_meaning.desc: Plant a detail in day 1–3 that seems incidental. Reference it later as significant: "Re-read the second paragraph of the letter we sent you on day 3." They find something they miss

parallel_threads.invite: person who is the link — the only one positioned to see the connection because both threads run through them
parallel_threads.desc: Three separate, seemingly unrelated contacts with a hidden shared element. The participant connects them before being told they're connected.

nomination_framing.invite: person who was selected by someone who saw something in them — the chosen
nomination_framing.desc: "You were recommended by someone who wishes to remain anonymous." / "Based on your [specific true thing], you were identified." / "Twelve people were considered.

dead_mans_switch.invite: person whose response matters — someone that something is waiting for
dead_mans_switch.desc: "If we don't receive confirmation by [specific date and time], the following will happen automatically." The impersonality — a system with its own logic that doesn't care about the

incomplete_sequence.invite: participant in something ongoing — person waiting for what comes next
incomplete_sequence.desc: "Part 1 of 2" in the subject line. An email that ends mid-sentence. A document with clearly missing sections. A voicemail that cuts off.

profiling_survey.invite: person whose profile is being built by something — someone whose specifics have been found relevant
profiling_survey.desc: Typeform or Google Forms. Frame as "required assessment before proceeding." Questions feel administrative; they're gathering dimensional data and calibration material.

reference_check_play.invite: candidate under evaluation by something that does its homework
reference_check_play.desc: An AI phone agent (or a human caller) contacts someone in the participant's life — a colleague, a friend, ideally someone professionally adjacent — and asks about the participant.

spotify_playlist.invite: person whose emotional state has been seen and curated — someone whose inner life was paid attention to
spotify_playlist.desc: A Spotify or Apple Music playlist shared from the fictional character's account. The song choices, order, and titles tell a story within the story.

rejection_gambit.invite: person who will prove they deserve access — someone with something to demonstrate
rejection_gambit.desc: After an application or intake-like interaction (designed or real), the participant receives a formal rejection.

false_breakthrough.invite: detective who cracked the case — and doesn't yet know there's more
false_breakthrough.desc: A planted "discovery" that appears to explain the mystery — the actor behind the evaluator, the LinkedIn that seems to expose the organization, the phone number that answers and co

simulated_loss.invite: person who had something they cared about and lost it — and must decide what that means
simulated_loss.desc: The participant is shown or told that something significant has been lost, destroyed, or compromised — finances drained, accounts locked, reputation damaged, a relationship betraye

fax.invite: person being reached by something from a different era — something that has been operating for a long time
fax.desc: Receiving a fax in 2026 is so unusual it creates immediate narrative weight.

telegram.invite: recipient of urgent, formal, official word from somewhere that still operates this way
telegram.desc: iTelegram.com delivers actual physical telegrams in Western Union format. ALL CAPS. STOP punctuation.

real_obituary.invite: person discovering that someone from the narrative has died — or didn't
real_obituary.desc: A fictional character's obituary in a real local paper — modest local and weekly papers have minimal verification requirements.

informed_delivery_ridealong.invite: person whose relationship with their own surveillance tool has been inverted

behavioral_ad.invite: person whose digital behavior has been observed and deliberately weaponized
behavioral_ad.desc: Upload the participant's email or phone to a Meta or Google custom audience. Run display or social ads from the narrative organization.

llm_contamination.invite: person who can't fully trust their own research tools — whose epistemic environment has been shaped
llm_contamination.desc: Plant content on the public web so that when the participant queries an AI search tool about the narrative entity, they get a result.

search_planted.invite: researcher who found the trail using the world's most trusted information channel
search_planted.desc: SEO-optimized content placed so that when the participant Googles a narrative-relevant name, phrase, or document title, they find it in the first page of results.

authenticity_proof.invite: person who just confirmed this is real — and now can't step back from that knowledge
authenticity_proof.desc: In an environment where everything could be AI-generated, demonstrating irrefutable realness is the counter-play.

ai_disclosure.invite: person navigating a world where human and machine are genuinely indistinguishable
ai_disclosure.desc: At a designed moment in a sustained character interaction, the operative (human or AI) explicitly says: "I should be transparent with you — I'm an AI system working on behalf of [o

notification_native.invite: person whose notification stream has been accessed — something is in the ambient layer of their attention
notification_native.desc: Delivery via push notification in the participant's existing app or browser ecosystem rather than SMS or email.

ai_character_interface.invite: person in an ongoing relationship with someone who is always there — an available confidant
ai_character_interface.desc: The fictional character is deployed as an actual AI interface the participant can talk to — a chatbot on the narrative domain, a custom GPT, a deployed LLM with a character system

personalization_uncanny.invite: person whose everyday life has been observed with actual care
personalization_uncanny.desc: A message in a format trained to be dismissed — marketing email, promotional SMS, "you have a new offer" notification — that is precisely, specifically personalized in ways that fo

corrupted_familiar.invite: person who noticed what others reading the same document would have missed
corrupted_familiar.desc: A small, specific discrepancy in something the participant regularly encounters and trusts. Not a forgery — a discrepancy. Their address with one digit wrong.

absent_record.invite: investigator who found where a record should exist and doesn't — witness to a deliberate absence
absent_record.desc: A gap where a record should exist. The absence is the artifact.

social_graph_leak.invite: person whose actual social world has been touched — not just their inbox or phone
social_graph_leak.desc: A real person already in the participant's network, briefed with exactly one detail and nothing else, who lets it surface naturally in an unrelated conversation.

simultaneous_convergence.invite: person who can see the pattern — the one at the center of coordinated attention that implies total reach
simultaneous_convergence.desc: The same reference arrives through multiple genuinely unconnected channels within a tight window — same day, sometimes the same hour.

hyperlocal_civic.invite: person whose physical world is being watched — not just their digital behavior
hyperlocal_civic.desc: A message delivered through the participant's actual neighborhood infrastructure — technically public, but specific enough to their location that coincidence is not a credible expl

real_system_entry.invite: investigator who found something that was already there before they looked — a record that required no one to place it for them
real_system_entry.desc: An actual entry created in a real public records system. Not a fake document — a genuine record in a genuine database, findable by anyone who searches, existing before the particip

retroactive_recontextualization.invite: person whose story was already in motion before they knew it — someone who has always been the protagonist
retroactive_recontextualization.desc: The participant's own real, publicly available history reframed as having been narrative-relevant before the experience began.

spatial_message.invite: person being observed in the physical world — not just the digital
spatial_message.desc: A message delivered through the participant's real physical environment with no digital component. No channel, no account, no deliverability problem.

the_witness.invite: person who has been seen by something in the physical world — and can't confirm or deny it
the_witness.desc: A stranger in a public space who seems to recognize the participant. Doesn't approach. Doesn't speak.

the_commission.invite: person who mattered enough to someone that they made something about them — a subject worthy of craft
the_commission.desc: An artifact that was clearly made specifically about the participant — not personalized, not customized, not addressed to them — made *about* them.

convincer_win.invite: The person whose instincts are right. The one who recognized a real opportunity.
convincer_win.desc: The foundational short-con mechanic: give the participant a small, real win early, before the stakes mean anything.

convincer_loss.invite: The person who doesn't quit when it gets hard. The one who sees it through.
convincer_loss.desc: The mirror of the convincer win and equally essential to the long con's architecture.

convincer_plant.invite: The one who finds what others miss. The investigator whose eyes are trained to see.
convincer_plant.desc: The art of arranging for the participant to "independently discover" fabricated evidence.

salting.invite: The one who notices. The person the world is speaking to.
salting.desc: Named directly after the mining fraud tradition: placing valuable ore in a depleted mine so an independent assayer would "discover" it and certify the mine as productive.

shill_validation.invite: The one who isn't alone in this. Part of a larger, select group who've seen the same thing.
shill_validation.desc: The shill is one of the oldest tools in con artistry. In three-card monte the shill wins visibly, recruiting bystanders into belief by demonstrating the game is winnable.

roper.invite: The one who was chosen by someone whose judgment they trust. Already inside a trusted relationship before the arc begins.
roper.desc: Maurer distinguishes the roper from other operatives with unusual care: the roper finds the mark and steers them toward the scenario without ever seeming to be part of it.

convincer_document.invite: The one who has been officially designated. Named. Documented. Part of something with a paper trail.
convincer_document.desc: The convincer document draws on the rich tradition of con artistry's paper infrastructure.

convincer_location.invite: The one who checked. Who didn't just take anyone's word for it. Who went and saw.
convincer_location.desc: The convincer location is the physical world's answer to document legitimacy.

convincer_crowd.invite: Member of a select group. Not alone. Part of a larger, real movement.
convincer_crowd.desc: In the big con's store, dozens of extras performed a convincing financial operation so the mark could see themselves as one of many sophisticated participants.

hurry_up.invite: The one who acts. Who doesn't hesitate when it matters. Who shows up.
hurry_up.desc: Maurer documents the hurry-up explicitly: "Don't let him think, don't let him talk to a lawyer, don't let him call his wife." The time pressure is artificial but its function is ps

spanish_prisoner.invite: The one who can help. The one who matters to someone in trouble. The one who won't abandon.
spanish_prisoner.desc: One of the oldest documented frauds — appearing in European records by the late 16th century.

pigeon_drop.invite: The trusted one. The keeper. The one who can hold something important without being told why.
pigeon_drop.desc: The pigeon drop's essential structure: two strangers find something valuable and propose to split it, but one must put up "good faith" collateral while waiting.

blow_off.invite: The one who completed something. Who saw it through. Who got out.
blow_off.desc: The blow-off is the con's exit mechanism — getting the mark away from the scene without suspicion or anger.

cooling_the_mark.invite: The one who was chosen for an experience rare enough to require this much craft. Not a victim — a subject.
cooling_the_mark.desc: Goffman's 1952 paper "On Cooling the Mark Out" is the foundational analysis.

inside_outside.invite: The one who can see both sides. The pivot point between two systems.
inside_outside.desc: The inside man / outside man is the structural heartbeat of the big con.

wire_convincer.invite: The one who is almost there. Who has come too far to stop now.
wire_convincer.desc: Named after the big con's climactic beat: "the wire" was a scheme in which the mark was convinced they had inside knowledge of delayed race results.

marks_pride.invite: The one who doesn't back down. Who shows up even when it's hard. Proof of who they say they are.
marks_pride.desc: The mark's pride is the mechanism that keeps marks from walking away at maximum doubt.

convincer_return.invite: The one who can see how it all connects. Who was in the story from the beginning.
convincer_return.desc: The callback — in improvisation, the planted callback; in narrative, Chekhov's gun; in con artistry, the moment when something the mark barely noticed returns as the key to the who

newspaper_test.invite: The careful one. The one who doesn't take things on faith. The one whose verification is meaningful.
newspaper_test.desc: Named after the journalist's fact-checking protocol — "if your mother says she loves you, check it." In con artistry, the newspaper test is the moment when the mark is directed to

cooling_letter.invite: The one who was seen. Who mattered. Who completed something.
cooling_letter.desc: Maurer describes the big con's cooler as a specialist — often the most emotionally skilled person in the crew — whose job was to talk a mark down into a state where they simply wen

planted_witness.invite: The investigator who found a witness. The one finding real people with real knowledge.
planted_witness.desc: The planted witness draws on the tradition of testimonial fraud — the use of apparently independent witnesses to corroborate a false account.

cold_read.invite: The one who has been accurately perceived. The one whose interior is not invisible.
cold_read.desc: The foundational technique of stage mentalists, psychic readers, and confidence artists who establish intimate knowledge of a stranger.

barnum_profile_dispatch.invite: You are someone whose inner complexity has rarely been accurately named.
barnum_profile_dispatch.desc: The Barnum or Forer effect names the phenomenon by which people accept vague, generally applicable statements as personally accurate when delivered with authority and apparent spec

cold_voicemail_drop.invite: You are someone whose path has been noticed, from outside your awareness.
cold_voicemail_drop.desc: A voicemail is left — apparently for the wrong number — by a confederate or agent-directed caller.

resonance_letter.invite: You are someone who has finally been seen by someone paying the right kind of attention.
resonance_letter.desc: The Resonance Letter is a Barnum profile in narrative disguise — delivered not as a psychological document but as a character's personal observation, written in first person, as th

sealed_envelope_reveal.invite: You are someone whose path was already written — and you walked it freely.
sealed_envelope_reveal.desc: A sealed envelope is delivered to the participant — by mail, courier, or in-person handoff — before any event or decision that the arc will subsequently engineer.

forced_choice_architecture.invite: You are someone who trusts their own instincts — your choices reveal something real about you.
forced_choice_architecture.desc: Forcing is the technical backbone of mentalism: engineering a participant's "free" choice so they select the pre-planned option while experiencing full subjective freedom.

one_ahead_sequence.invite: You are someone who is just now understanding that the sequence was always ahead of you.
one_ahead_sequence.desc: The one-ahead principle: each piece of information the system reveals about the participant appears to have been known before it was disclosed.

coincidence_stack.invite: You are someone whose life has a structure that is only now becoming visible.
coincidence_stack.desc: Three unconnected coincidences, engineered to arrive within a 48-hour window, using different channels and apparent origins. Each one alone is dismissible. Two create interest.

ambient_number_seeding.invite: You are someone beginning to notice that certain things recur in your life for reasons you haven't yet named.
ambient_number_seeding.desc: A number, word, symbol, or image is placed in the participant's environment repeatedly — across different contexts, through apparently unconnected channels — in the days or weeks b

progressive_anesthesia.invite: You are someone who goes further than most — not recklessly, but because you recognize there is more.
progressive_anesthesia.desc: Derren Brown's principle of progressive anesthesia — derived from his analysis of how compliance, disclosure, and emotional investment can be escalated without resistance when each

dual_reality_dispatch.invite: You are someone having an experience that others are having differently — and you are not sure what that means about what is real.
dual_reality_dispatch.desc: Two participants receive different versions of the same interaction.

retroactive_biography.invite: You are someone who has been known — without your knowledge — by someone paying close, quiet attention across time.
retroactive_biography.desc: The character running the experience delivers evidence — a photograph, a document, a letter, a reference — that they have known about the participant longer than the timeline of th

thoughtform_plant.invite: You are someone who, once given a lens, sees through it everywhere — because the lens was already yours.
thoughtform_plant.desc: Early in the arc, the participant is given a symbol, word, or image — apparently casually, as a small gift or curiosity — and told only that it is "something worth holding." No exp

async_book_test.invite: You are someone whose private interior — even the random choices you make alone — is somehow legible to this system.
async_book_test.desc: The classic book test — in which a mentalist appears to know the word a participant chose at random from a book — is adapted for async delivery through a combination of guided choi

ambient_symbol_echo.invite: You are someone in whose life certain things repeat — not by chance.
ambient_symbol_echo.desc: A symbol established early in the arc — in a letter, a gift, a casual communication — subsequently appears in multiple unconnected contexts throughout the arc's duration.

intake_echo.invite: You are someone who has already told this system more than you realized — and it listened carefully.
intake_echo.desc: Information disclosed during intake — by the sponsor, by the participant in calibration, or inferred from behavioral signals during early arc beats — surfaces in a later communicat

filed_prediction.invite: You are someone whose story was known — and confirmed — before you lived it.
filed_prediction.desc: A prediction is made — in writing — and filed with an apparently independent third party before the experience begins: a lawyer, a notary, a trusted institution, or a friend of the

directed_dream.invite: You are someone whose inner life — even the parts that operate without your direction — is part of this process.
directed_dream.desc: Late in the day — optimally within two hours of the participant's typical sleep time — a communication is delivered that gives the participant an instruction, object, or prompt to

third_party_confirmation.invite: You are someone whose private read on the situation was right — and someone else has arrived at the same place independently.
third_party_confirmation.desc: A confederate — playing either a stranger or a character with apparent independence from the arc — confirms the participant's private suspicion without being asked.

self_fulfilling_dispatch.invite: You are someone who will do what you are told you will do — because it is already true of you.
self_fulfilling_dispatch.desc: The participant is told — in character voice, with confidence — what they will "naturally" do next.

named_stranger.invite: You are someone for whom the world has arranged this meeting — and you recognized it when it came.
named_stranger.desc: The participant is told — with appropriate gravity and minimal explanation — that they will encounter a person with a specific first name or role ("you will meet someone named Mart

calibrated_miss.invite: You are someone paying close enough attention to notice when something is almost right — and then to see how it corrects.
calibrated_miss.desc: A deliberate near-miss: the agent makes a claim that is close but not quite accurate, then corrects it.

threshold_naming.invite: You are someone who has earned a name for what you have become during this arc.
threshold_naming.desc: At the arc's threshold moment — when the participant has crossed the central challenge or transformation the arc was built around — they are given a word or name that describes the

legend_seed.invite: the person who was already being watched before they knew it
legend_seed.desc: A legend is a false but documented identity — an entity with a past.

walk_in_approach.invite: the person significant enough to be found
walk_in_approach.desc: In real HUMINT, a walk-in is an unsolicited volunteer who presents themselves to an intelligence service with an offer.

brush_pass_transfer.desc: A brush pass is a physical exchange between two people who appear to have no relationship, executed in motion, lasting under three seconds.

dead_drop_receive.invite: the operative who knows how to receive without being seen
dead_drop_receive.desc: The participant receives instructions directing them to a specific public location and a specific retrieval method: the third book on the second shelf from the bottom of a particul

challenge_response_protocol.invite: the operative who knows the codes
challenge_response_protocol.desc: The participant is given a challenge phrase and its response in an early contact — framed as authentication protocol, the way two strangers who are both in the operation can verify

cut_out_introduction.invite: the operative who accepts that not everything is visible
cut_out_introduction.desc: A cut-out is an intermediary who operates between two parties who do not communicate directly, so that neither party can fully expose the other.

mice_ego_appeal.invite: the person who was worth finding
mice_ego_appeal.desc: The MICE framework identifies four primary recruitment vectors for human intelligence assets.

mice_ideology_appeal.invite: the person whose values are a form of capability
mice_ideology_appeal.desc: The ideology recruitment vector does not appeal to what the participant wants — it appeals to what they believe.

mice_coercion_shadow.invite: the person who knows how to move carefully
mice_coercion_shadow.desc: This play is the most ethically weighted in this section and should be deployed only with deliberate care.

mice_money_signal.invite: the person who knows that everything has a price, including their particular skills
mice_money_signal.desc: The material recruitment vector works by making the exchange explicit: the entity offers something of value in return for the participant's cooperation.

surveillance_detection_route.invite: the operative who moves through the world like they know they might be followed
surveillance_detection_route.desc: A surveillance detection route is a specific path through public space designed to expose anyone following the operative without appearing to look for surveillance.

mole_hunt_brief.invite: the investigator who suspects everyone and must work anyway
mole_hunt_brief.desc: The participant is briefed by a handler or principal: there is a leak in the operation, someone close to the participant is passing information to an adversary, and the participant

compartmentalization_brief.invite: the person trusted with the part others are not permitted to see
compartmentalization_brief.desc: The participant is told, in specific operational terms, that they are being given information on a need-to-know basis.

legend_stress_test.invite: the operative who can hold the legend under pressure
legend_stress_test.desc: The participant has been given a cover story as part of their role in the operation — biographical details, a backstory, a set of facts about who they are in the context of the arc

exfiltration_protocol.desc: The participant receives a document — brief, operationally terse — that describes what they should do if their cover is blown or contact is lost.

legend_depth_build.invite: the person who has been part of something longer than they knew
legend_depth_build.desc: While legend_seed plants a legend for a character, this play creates discovered evidence that the operation — or the participant's own role within it — has a history that predates

asset_validation_request.invite: the asset whose knowledge is worth something
asset_validation_request.desc: The participant is asked to confirm or evaluate a specific piece of information: Does this person's description match someone they know? Is this address consistent with what they u

dry_cleaning_walk.invite: the operative who moves through the city as if their life depends on getting it right
dry_cleaning_walk.desc: Dry cleaning is the process of systematically removing surveillance before an operational meeting or dead drop.

one_time_pad_receipt.invite: the operative who communicates in a channel no one can intercept
one_time_pad_receipt.desc: The participant receives a physical object: a card or small booklet containing a one-time pad — a grid of letters or numbers, or a simpler keyed system designed for the arc's techn

handler_letter.invite: the asset who has a handler who knows who they actually are
handler_letter.desc: A handler letter is a piece of direct communication from the handler figure to the participant — warm in register, operationally grounded, implying an ongoing relationship that has

safe_house_assignment.invite: the operative who knows where safety is
safe_house_assignment.desc: The participant is given an address — a real, publicly accessible location in their city — and told it is a fallback point if contact is lost or their situation becomes untenable.

mice_full_profile.invite: the subject who now knows they were studied
mice_full_profile.desc: This is the section's highest-intensity play and must be treated as a threshold event rather than a routine deployment.

welcome_flood.invite: someone worth attending to; someone already known and valued before they knew they were known
welcome_flood.desc: The play opens the arc. In a short window — 24 to 72 hours after first real contact — the participant receives multiple signals of recognition across more than one channel.

lexical_handshake.invite: someone who speaks this language; an insider
lexical_handshake.desc: Communications begin using two to four specific terms that carry meaning within the arc's world.

the_vetting_letter.invite: someone who has been evaluated and found to possess something real; the chosen
the_vetting_letter.desc: A formal letter — ideally physical, on stock that signals seriousness — informs the participant that they have been considered against a set of criteria and found to be someone the

first_disclosure_invitation.invite: someone with an interior worth knowing; someone trusted enough to receive a rare truth
first_disclosure_invitation.desc: The organization asks the participant to share something true about themselves that they rarely share — not their deepest secret, but something real that does not usually come up.

incremental_oath.invite: someone who keeps their word; someone who has made a series of real choices to be here
incremental_oath.desc: A series of requests, each small, each slightly larger than the last, each framed as natural next steps. The first might be to respond to a question.

lexical_deepening.invite: someone fluent in a private world; someone whose perception has been refined
lexical_deepening.desc: Where the lexical handshake introduced two to four terms, lexical deepening introduces vocabulary that reframes.

the_naming_ceremony.invite: someone with a title, a role, a name within this world; a person who has been formally recognized
the_naming_ceremony.desc: The participant is given a name, title, designation, or epithet by the organization.

milieu_letter.invite: someone who now understands the shape of the world they have entered
milieu_letter.desc: A letter — or document formatted as a letter — that describes the world the participant has entered. Not a handbook. A vision statement written for one reader.

the_shadow_walk.invite: someone being watched over; someone worth accompanying
the_shadow_walk.desc: The participant is told, gently and warmly, that someone in the organization has been paying attention to them — not watching in a surveillance sense, but holding them in mind, not

the_purity_protocol.invite: someone who does what this requires; someone who has made their commitment legible
the_purity_protocol.desc: The organization asks the participant to do one specific, slightly unusual thing as a mark of their commitment: abstain from something for a defined period, perform a small ritual

the_deep_archive_pull.invite: someone whose history matters; someone whose story has been attended to long before they knew it
the_deep_archive_pull.desc: The organization demonstrates — through reference to specific, accurate, pre-arc information — that it has records, attention, or knowledge of the participant that predates first c

thought_stopping_interrupt.invite: someone whose doubt is a sign of depth rather than a reason to stop
thought_stopping_interrupt.desc: When the participant expresses doubt, confusion, or resistance, the response does not engage the doubt directly. It does not argue, does not dismiss, and does not ignore.

the_floating_moment.invite: someone who has touched something real; someone who now knows what is possible
the_floating_moment.desc: A brief, intense, carefully staged experience that produces something close to transcendence: a moment of genuine felt connection with another person, a sudden perception of beauty

witnessed_confession.invite: someone who has been fully seen and not turned away from; someone whose interior was witnessed and honored
witnessed_confession.desc: The participant is invited to share something genuinely vulnerable — not performed vulnerability, but something that carries weight, something they have not easily shared.

the_us_signal.invite: someone who understands something most people don't; someone on the inside of something real
the_us_signal.desc: A communication that draws a gentle, clear line between the participant and people who have not had this experience or developed this understanding.

graduated_reveal.invite: someone who earns their way deeper; someone whose readiness has been recognized
graduated_reveal.desc: Information about the arc's world is released in tiers, each requiring something from the participant before it becomes available.

graduation_ritual.invite: someone who has crossed; someone who is now different from who they were at entry
graduation_ritual.desc: A threshold is crossed. Something marks it — a physical object delivered, a ceremony conducted, a specific phrase spoken by the right person in the right context, a document signed

the_long_echo.invite: someone who has been accompanied the whole time; someone whose story was known before they knew it
the_long_echo.desc: A phrase, image, symbol, or object from the very opening of the experience reappears, unexpectedly, near the end. Not as repetition — as return. The participant recognizes it.

parallel_correspondent.invite: someone among others; part of something larger, even if the larger remains out of reach
parallel_correspondent.desc: The participant learns that there is someone else going through a similar journey — a correspondent, a parallel traveler, someone who has been asking similar questions or facing si

borrowed_authority.invite: someone participating in something that serious, credible people have taken seriously
borrowed_authority.desc: The organization invokes a respected figure — historical, institutional, artistic, intellectual — as a witness to the questions or purposes the arc engages.

productive_rupture.invite: someone who has tested this and found it real; someone who stayed through something difficult
productive_rupture.desc: Something goes wrong. The organization fails the participant in a way that is real enough to hurt — a missed communication, an apparent betrayal, a promise broken, an unexpected si

exit_honored.invite: someone whose choice is respected unconditionally; someone who was never trapped
exit_honored.desc: When the participant uses their safety word, slows significantly, or signals in any way that they wish to disengage, the organization responds immediately and with warmth.

anomalous_receipt.invite: the person who notices what others would discard
anomalous_receipt.desc: A piece of mail or email arrives addressed to someone else — a name close to but not the participant's, or a previous resident, or a name that will later prove meaningful.

dead_letter_office.invite: the inheritor of an interrupted story
dead_letter_office.desc: A package or thick envelope arrives with visible evidence of travel: multiple forwarding address labels layered over each other, a postmark dated three to six weeks prior, handling

cipher_in_plain_sight.invite: the analyst who sees structure where others see noise
cipher_in_plain_sight.desc: A message is hidden inside something public and unremarkable: a classified ad in a local paper, a product description in an online shop, a public event listing, a block of legal bo

multi_key_first_fragment.invite: the investigator at the beginning of something real
multi_key_first_fragment.desc: A first piece of evidence arrives that is clearly not the whole picture.

time_locked_page.invite: the initiate who has been granted access at the appointed time
time_locked_page.desc: A URL is passed to the participant — embedded in a document, spoken by a character, found at the end of a cipher — with the information that it will be accessible "after Thursday"

fragmented_witness.invite: the arbiter of truth from partial testimony
fragmented_witness.desc: Three or more partial accounts of the same event arrive through different channels and across different time points.

the_unreliable_archive.invite: the investigator who cannot outsource judgment
the_unreliable_archive.desc: A dossier arrives — physical binder, PDF, or accessible archive site — containing documents that purport to be a complete record of something: a case file, a person's correspondenc

puppet_master_near_miss.invite: the witness to the seam between worlds
puppet_master_near_miss.desc: A moment is engineered where the participant gets a partial glimpse behind the production. A character breaks slightly from their script and then recovers.

tinag_frame_break.invite: the participant who has been found out by the world they're investigating
tinag_frame_break.desc: A moment occurs where the experience acknowledges that the participant knows they are in an experience — but does so through a character or mechanism inside the fiction.

optional_path.invite: the person who goes further than required
optional_path.desc: A fork appears in the material — clearly marked, or clearly implied by the structure — where one branch is the expected continuation and another is available but not required.

community_solve_bait.invite: the analyst who may not be alone in this
community_solve_bait.desc: A puzzle or evidence set arrives that is genuinely difficult for one person to solve alone — not impossibly so, but requiring either breadth of reference knowledge that any single

the_false_confirmation.invite: the investigator whose intuitions were right before they knew it
the_false_confirmation.desc: A message arrives — from a character, from a system, from an implied source — that confirms a theory the participant has not yet fully formed.

meta_game_layer.invite: the investigator who has discovered that the map has a map
meta_game_layer.desc: A moment in the experience reveals that there is a structural layer above the one the participant has been operating in.

slow_burn_url.invite: the reader who held on to something before knowing why
slow_burn_url.desc: A URL appears in the very first or second contact — embedded in a letter, visible in a photograph, listed at the bottom of a receipt as if it were a terms-of-service link.

personalized_glitch.invite: the person who has been noticed by something vast
personalized_glitch.desc: An anomaly appears in a system the participant uses daily — and the anomaly is specifically calibrated to them.

relay_handler.invite: the initiated traveler passing between guides
relay_handler.desc: The participant is passed from one character or handler to another.

physical_object_as_key.invite: the holder of something that was meant to reach them
physical_object_as_key.desc: A physical object arrives — mailed or placed — that is itself functional, not representational. Not a letter about a key, but an actual key.

breadcrumb_sequence.invite: the investigator whose own effort moves the story
breadcrumb_sequence.desc: A series of discoveries, each one containing the next step. The participant receives something — a document, an object, a message — that contains or implies a direction: check this

witness_who_returns.invite: the investigator whose earlier encounter now makes sense
witness_who_returns.desc: A character who appeared briefly and apparently peripherally in early material — a name on a cc: list, a brief phone call from someone who said they'd be in touch, a mention in a l

diegetic_archive_site.invite: the researcher with access to the primary source
diegetic_archive_site.desc: A website exists that is a narrative object inside the fiction — the internal document repository of a fictional organization, a character's personal archive, the case files of an

scheduled_interruption.invite: the person being kept in contact by something that tracks time
scheduled_interruption.desc: The participant has been told, by a character or through found material, that something will arrive on a specific day: "Expect something Wednesday." Wednesday arrives.

convergence_point.invite: the investigator who has assembled the picture
convergence_point.desc: Multiple threads from different channels — letters, a website, phone calls, a physical object, a character's communications — all point to the same location, person, date, or momen

wax_seal_letter.desc: A letter arrives on heavy cotton paper — 24lb or above, cream or off-white — with a wax seal on the back flap.

one_wrong_word.invite: the reader whose attention is the instrument
one_wrong_word.desc: A document, letter, or message arrives that is entirely correct — plausible, consistent, well-written — except for one word that is wrong in a specific and non-accidental way.

retro_medium_artifact.invite: the archivist who found something that was never meant to be found; the one whose attention discovered what casual viewers missed
retro_medium_artifact.desc: A digital or physical artifact that appears to come from an older, more authentic medium — VHS aesthetics with CRT scan lines and tracking errors, a GeoCities-era HTML page hosted

soft_landing.invite: the person who came through something real
soft_landing.desc: The gap this addresses: participants emerge from climax in an altered state — adrenaline, emotion, uncertainty about what just happened — and the most common design failure is fill

artifact_as_relic.invite: the person who has been somewhere worth remembering
artifact_as_relic.desc: The gap this addresses: physical objects passed during the arc (a key, a letter, a photograph, a coin) are experienced in context — they mean what the arc told them to mean in the

day_seven_reframe.invite: the person who now understands something they didn't know they were learning
day_seven_reframe.desc: The gap this addresses: participants reflect on an immersive experience differently at seven days than they do at zero.

integration_letter.invite: the person who demonstrated something specific and real about themselves
integration_letter.desc: The gap this addresses: most immersive experiences give participants an experience but not a witness.

long_shadow.invite: the person this character still thinks about
long_shadow.desc: The gap this addresses: thirty days post-denouement, most participants have metabolized the experience and filed it — as memory, as story, as something that happened.

quiet_withdrawal.invite: the person whose story has concluded (not been interrupted)
quiet_withdrawal.desc: The gap this addresses: silence after an immersive experience reads as abandonment unless it is designed.

graceful_release.invite: the person whose choice to leave is respected completely
graceful_release.desc: The gap this addresses: most resistance-handling frameworks are implicitly about re-engagement — they treat a participant checking out as a problem to be solved.

open_door_signal.invite: the person who can return on their own terms
open_door_signal.desc: The gap this addresses: there is a class of participant who is not fully out but has gone quiet — they may be overwhelmed, busy, uncertain, or processing something the arc surfaced

breadcrumb_without_demand.invite: the person who is still worth thinking about, even without reciprocating
breadcrumb_without_demand.desc: The gap this addresses: a participant who has checked out but not explicitly exited is in a particular state — they have withdrawn investment but probably still have ambient awaren

seam_acknowledgment.invite: the person perceptive enough to notice what most people miss
seam_acknowledgment.desc: The gap this addresses: a participant who finds a seam — a Google-able actor, a domain registered this year, a phone number that doesn't match the geography — is at a critical deci

test_reframe.invite: the person who was right — and whose rightness reveals something more interesting than they expected
test_reframe.desc: The gap this addresses: "Am I being tested?" is one of the most common and structurally useful moments in any high-immersion experience.

misfire_as_story.invite: the person whose experience was coherent after all
misfire_as_story.desc: The gap this addresses: plays misfire. A letter arrives too late, a confederate improvises badly, a tone is misjudged, a contact lands in the wrong emotional window.

wrong_read_recovery.invite: the person who is being paid close enough attention to warrant a correction
wrong_read_recovery.desc: The gap this addresses: agent or confederate misread the participant's state — delivered an escalation play when the participant was depleted, sent a playful beat during grief, pus

shadow_correspondent.invite: the person complex enough to be read differently by different people
shadow_correspondent.desc: The gap this addresses: when the system has misread a participant and the primary character is now slightly out of alignment, the shadow correspondent appears as a second character

broken_immersion_honor.invite: the person brave enough to ask what's real, and steady enough to hear the answer
broken_immersion_honor.desc: The gap this addresses: a participant who explicitly breaks the fourth wall — "I know this isn't real," "who are you actually," "I want to talk to the person running this" — is not

elegant_failure.invite: the person who now understands the system better because it showed its strain
elegant_failure.desc: The gap this addresses: physical and logistical failures happen — wrong envelope, letter to wrong address, call at wrong time, number no longer in service.

unknowing_collaboration.invite: the person whose choices were consequential in ways they didn't know
unknowing_collaboration.desc: The gap this addresses: single-participant arcs are inherently solipsistic — the world of the experience is organized entirely around one person.

parallel_discovery.invite: the person who was not the only one looking
parallel_discovery.desc: The gap this addresses: discovery in a single-participant arc is private — only this person found this thing, only this person knows this.

convergence_signal.invite: the person whose journey was part of something larger than they knew
convergence_signal.desc: The gap this addresses: the convergence_signal is the delivery mechanism for the revelation that a participant's arc was not solo.

ambient_organization_presence.invite: the person who has found something real and verifiable
ambient_organization_presence.desc: The gap this addresses: an organization or character that exists only within the contacts sent to the participant collapses the moment they open a second tab.

deep_backstory_artifacts.invite: the person who digs and finds something older than they expected
deep_backstory_artifacts.desc: The gap this addresses: a world that only exists from first contact forward has no depth.

dormant_frequency.invite: the person who held on to something without knowing why, and was right to
dormant_frequency.desc: The gap this addresses: channels introduced during the arc's early stages and then silenced create an ambient background condition — the participant may periodically wonder about t

unmarked_anniversary.invite: the one whose history is known and held by something outside themselves
unmarked_anniversary.desc: A message arrives on the exact calendar date of a significant event the participant disclosed — the day a relationship ended, the anniversary of a death, the date they left a city

calendar_holding.invite: someone whose timeline is a landscape someone else is also walking through
calendar_holding.desc: During intake, the agent assembles a private calendar of dates significant to the participant: birthdays of people they've lost, departure dates, founding dates of things they buil

first_anniversary_dispatch.invite: someone who has lived a year with something, and is still here
first_anniversary_dispatch.desc: A letter or card arrives one year to the day after a significant arc event, or after a loss the participant disclosed.

elegiac_obituary.invite: someone who loved something enough to mourn it properly
elegiac_obituary.desc: An obituary-format document arrives for something the participant has lost — not a person (that is the existing real_obituary play), but the relational and experiential losses that

time_capsule_dispatch.invite: someone who can stand at the hinge between who they were and who they are and find both worth addressing
time_capsule_dispatch.desc: A letter arrives written in the voice of the participant's past self, addressed to their present self.

photograph_stranger_knows_you.invite: someone who is known across distances they don't understand
photograph_stranger_knows_you.desc: A photograph arrives — an archival or found-photography image of someone the participant has never met.

last_known_address_letter.invite: someone whose former homes still hold a part of them
last_known_address_letter.desc: A letter is sent to an address the participant no longer lives at. The letter is addressed to them by name, at that address.

locate_photograph_assignment.invite: the investigator of their own history, returning to the scene
locate_photograph_assignment.desc: The participant is asked to locate something that still exists from a period or relationship they have lost and to photograph it.

letter_never_sent.invite: someone with the courage to speak to what cannot hear them — and to be witnessed doing it
letter_never_sent.desc: The participant is asked to write a letter they never sent — to a person, a place, a former self, a lost relationship, or something they never got to say goodbye to.

small_ceremony_instruction.invite: someone who knows how to mark what matters, who holds their own ceremonies
small_ceremony_instruction.desc: A set of instructions arrives for a small, specific ceremony. Light a candle at a specific time. Open a window and leave it open for one hour. Set a place at the table.

liminal_hour_dispatch.invite: someone who receives transmissions others cannot, at hours when the boundary thins
liminal_hour_dispatch.desc: A message is deployed at a liminal time — 3:07am, or at the exact minute of local sunset, or at astronomical twilight, or at the moment the day turns.

natural_calendar_beat.invite: someone who lives by a calendar older than the one on their phone
natural_calendar_beat.desc: A message arrives timed to a natural calendrical event — the summer solstice, the winter solstice, a new moon, a full moon, an equinox — at the precise moment the event occurs at t

absent_presence_character.invite: someone who is trusted by the dead, or chosen by someone who cannot choose them anymore
absent_presence_character.desc: A character exists in the arc who died before the arc began. They are not a ghost — no supernatural claims are made.

voice_from_disconnected_number.invite: someone who receives transmissions from beyond the ordinary reach of communication
voice_from_disconnected_number.desc: A voicemail arrives from a number the participant does not recognize. When they call it back — and they will call it back — the number is disconnected.

past_tense_prophecy.invite: someone who has lived through the thing they are living through — who has the knowledge of having survived
past_tense_prophecy.desc: A message arrives written entirely in past tense about events that have not yet happened — but which are plausible, near-future extensions of the participant's current life.

memorial_object_arrival.invite: someone who has been thought of, who has received something made or chosen with care
memorial_object_arrival.desc: A small physical object arrives with no explanation — or with only the slimmest explanation, a card with a word or a date or a brief phrase that does not fully decode what the obje

year_mind_return.invite: someone whose transformation was witnessed and is still held
year_mind_return.desc: Exactly one year after arc completion — or after a specific beat identified at arc design time as the "year's mind moment" — a brief communication arrives.

inventory_of_what_remains.invite: the careful archivist of their own life — someone who notices and names what endures
inventory_of_what_remains.desc: The participant is asked to make a list. Not of what they have lost — but of what remains.

forwarded_grief.invite: someone who is a link in a chain of mourning they did not start and will not end
forwarded_grief.desc: A document arrives that appears to have passed through other hands. A letter folded with fold-marks from other openings.

sealed_room.invite: someone who decides when they are ready — who does not have to grieve on loss's schedule
sealed_room.desc: The participant is told — within the arc's fiction — that there is a room where the things that are too much to hold right now have been placed. The room is sealed.

witness_register.invite: someone whose losses are real enough to be recorded — whose grief counts
witness_register.desc: The participant is invited to enter a loss into a register — a formal record maintained by the arc's institution, character, or system.

grief_time_legible.invite: someone who has carried something a long way and can now see the shape of what they carried
grief_time_legible.desc: A document arrives that looks back at the arc's grief-register beats from a point of sufficient distance. Not a summary — a retrospection.

memo_field_transmission.invite: the person whose attention is being sought by something that knows where to reach them
memo_field_transmission.desc: A micro-payment between $0.01 and $0.99 is sent to the participant via Venmo, PayPal, Zelle, or Cash App from a freshly created account with a plausible but non-searchable name.

receipt_anomaly_line_item.invite: the person who notices what others walk past
receipt_anomaly_line_item.desc: A cooperating vendor — coffee shop, bookstore, hardware store, any business the participant already patronizes — adds a single anomalous line item to the participant's receipt for

refund_from_nowhere.invite: the person someone thought to repay
refund_from_nowhere.desc: A PayPal Business payment is sent to the participant labeled as a refund — using the "refund" transaction type where available, or a transfer with an explicit memo: "Refund — Accou

invoice_intangible_services.invite: the person who has been receiving something they didn't account for
invoice_intangible_services.desc: A professionally formatted invoice arrives — via email from a domain the operator controls, or as a PDF attached to a plain-text email, or printed and mailed via USPS.

statement_of_account.invite: the person who has been running a ledger they didn't know existed
statement_of_account.desc: A multi-page account statement mailed or emailed as a PDF covers a fictional "account history" for the participant.

escrow_formal_notice.invite: the person whose choices have monetary consequence — even their inner ones
escrow_formal_notice.desc: A formal notice on institutional letterhead informs the participant that a specific sum — between $15 and $200, chosen for narrative significance — has been placed in escrow pendin

beneficiary_selection_notice.invite: the person who was selected — who was seen by someone keeping a longer account than they realized
beneficiary_selection_notice.desc: A letter arrives informing the participant that they have been designated as a beneficiary for a disbursement from a named fund or trust.

grant_award_letter.invite: the practitioner whose work has been seen and valued by the right people
grant_award_letter.desc: A formal award letter arrives — on letterhead, in an envelope, with a real stamp — from a plausible arts, research, or community foundation.

bearer_bond_certificate.invite: the shareholder — the one who holds a stake in something others don't know is real
bearer_bond_certificate.desc: A formal stock certificate or bearer bond arrives by mail — a physical document, 8.5" × 11" or larger, with the visual language of early-to-mid twentieth century securities: ornate

ticker_feed_events.invite: the investor tracking something real that no one else can see
ticker_feed_events.desc: A custom web page is built and maintained — minimal, financial-style design — that displays a market ticker for one or more fictional assets.

portfolio_life_decisions.invite: the person whose life has been, all along, an investment strategy — one worth analyzing
portfolio_life_decisions.desc: A portfolio report — formatted as a private wealth management document — maps the participant's actual life decisions to notional financial performance.

commodity_narrative_report.invite: the analyst who reads the market for what it actually means
commodity_narrative_report.desc: A commodity market report arrives — formatted as an industry brief, the kind distributed to futures traders, agricultural planners, or weather derivatives desks.

collections_agency_notice.invite: the person who must account for what they owe — even the debts they didn't know they were running
collections_agency_notice.desc: A debt collection letter arrives — formatted with full fidelity to the genre: "NOTICE OF PAST-DUE ACCOUNT. This communication is from a debt collector.

promissory_note.invite: the person who makes formal promises — who takes their own commitments seriously enough to sign them
promissory_note.desc: A promissory note arrives by mail — formatted as a legal instrument, single page, with the spare precision of real financial notes.

lien_on_abstract.invite: the person who must defend something they didn't know they owned
lien_on_abstract.desc: A lien notice arrives — formatted as a UCC Uniform Commercial Code filing notice or a state-level lien notification, the kind that would accompany a mechanic's lien or an encumbran

credit_report_parallel_self.invite: the person who chose this life, knowing another was possible
credit_report_parallel_self.desc: A credit report arrives — formatted as an Equifax, Experian, or TransUnion consumer disclosure.

w2_fictional_employer.invite: the professional whose most important work was always already employment, even when it went unpaid
w2_fictional_employer.desc: An IRS Form W-2 — formatted identically to the real document, using the current year's layout available from IRS.gov for reference — arrives in an envelope.

property_tax_notice.invite: the person who has always held a claim on a particular place — even without the deed
property_tax_notice.desc: A property tax notice arrives — formatted as a county assessor or municipal tax authority document — for a real property that the participant has a relationship to but does not own

appraisal_letter.invite: the person whose choices have market value — whose judgment has been assessed and found worth something
appraisal_letter.desc: An appraisal letter arrives from a fictional appraisal or advisory firm, formatted as a professional written appraisal of a specific item.

market_analysis_participant_choices.invite: the rational actor who has access to better information than they realized
market_analysis_participant_choices.desc: A market analysis document arrives — formatted as a financial advisory brief, the kind distributed to private clients by boutique investment advisors or family offices.

dividend_notice.invite: the patient investor — the person who held something and is now receiving a return
dividend_notice.desc: A dividend notice arrives — a brief, formal document notifying the participant that a dividend has been declared and paid on their holdings.

inheritance_fraction_notice.invite: the inheritor — the person who carries something forward, whether they chose to or not
inheritance_fraction_notice.desc: A letter arrives from a fictional estate attorney — on letterhead, by USPS, with a real stamp and a return address in a plausible city.

pre_sleep_sensory_dispatch.invite: the one who pays attention to what happens at the edges
pre_sleep_sensory_dispatch.desc: The dispatch arrives between 60 and 30 minutes before the participant's tracked or self-reported sleep time — close enough to bed that it travels with them but not so close that it

sleep_data_mirror.invite: someone whose inner life has a shape that can be read
sleep_data_mirror.desc: If the participant uses a sleep-tracking wearable (Fitbit, Oura, Apple Watch, Garmin), the agent requests read access to their sleep data through the platform's public API with par

three_am_dispatch.invite: someone who receives transmissions at the edges of consciousness
three_am_dispatch.desc: Scheduled to arrive between 2:45 and 3:15 AM local time, pushed as a phone notification that is brief enough to be read half-asleep in under ten seconds.

dream_report_request.invite: someone whose inner life produces material worth attending to
dream_report_request.desc: The request is made the evening before, so it travels with the participant into sleep: "Before you sleep: if you remember anything from tonight — image, feeling, fragment — send it

hypnagogic_image_seed.invite: someone whose unconscious processes are in contact with something larger
hypnagogic_image_seed.desc: The image seed is a specific, concrete visual or sensory description sent within the participant's pre-sleep window.

breath_gate.desc: A message arrives that cannot be opened immediately. Instead, it contains a brief instruction: "Before you read this, complete four cycles of the following: breathe in for four cou

somatic_key_installation.invite: someone who carries something the body knows
somatic_key_installation.desc: The installation happens in three stages across the arc's first third.

stress_state_calibration.invite: someone whose physical state is worth knowing
stress_state_calibration.desc: For seven consecutive days, the participant receives a prompt at the same time daily — chosen to be the moment in their day when they are most likely to be both accessible and tran

contemplative_walk_instruction.invite: someone who moves through the world as a practice
contemplative_walk_instruction.desc: The instruction specifies three parameters: time of day, pace, and one sensory constraint.

carry_this.invite: someone who carries something that matters
carry_this.desc: The participant is asked to carry a specific small object — one they already own — for a defined period, typically one to two weeks.

home_environment_task.invite: someone who prepares a space for what is coming
home_environment_task.desc: The participant is asked to make one specific, physical change to their home environment. Not their entire home — one location, one action.

threshold_crossing_practice.invite: someone who notices when one state becomes another
threshold_crossing_practice.desc: For 48 hours, the participant is asked to notice every time they cross a threshold — every doorway, every curb, every elevator arrival, every gate, every transition from one space

sensory_diary.invite: someone who attends to the texture of experience
sensory_diary.desc: Each day for a week, the participant is asked to send one sensory detail: "One thing you noticed today with your body — not your eyes.

temperature_weather_dispatch.invite: someone whose body is in relationship with the world's body
temperature_weather_dispatch.desc: The agent monitors the participant's local weather via a public weather API (OpenWeatherMap or equivalent) and uses three variables to time and calibrate dispatches: current temper

anticipatory_signal_state.invite: someone who is waiting for something real
anticipatory_signal_state.desc: The participant is told that a specific signal will arrive within the next 72 hours.

hunger_window_delivery.invite: someone who receives in a state of need
hunger_window_delivery.desc: The play exploits a well-documented physiological phenomenon: in a fasted state (approximately four or more hours after eating), and in the immediate post-exercise window, several

circadian_dispatch_series.invite: someone whose day has a rhythm that is attended to
circadian_dispatch_series.desc: The arc maps to the participant's circadian rhythm: wake-time dispatch, midday dispatch, evening dispatch, pre-sleep dispatch.

seasonal_body_dispatch.invite: someone whose body is part of a larger cycle
