seasonal_body_dispatch.desc: The play establishes a relationship between the arc's content and the season's specific somatic signature.

dusk_dawn_dispatch.invite: someone who is present at the moment of change
dusk_dawn_dispatch.desc: The dispatch is timed to arrive at the precise moment of local sunset or local sunrise, calculated via the participant's city and the date.

physical_threshold_unlock.invite: someone whose body can do what is asked of it
physical_threshold_unlock.desc: A specific arc beat — a piece of content, an instruction, a revelation — is placed behind a physical threshold.

indexed_ghost_page.invite: someone whose name or life has been noted by an unknown party
indexed_ghost_page.desc: Create a real, indexable webpage on a platform the participant has no reason to monitor — a minor subdomain of a plausible organization, a GitHub Pages site for a fictional researc

archive_snapshot.invite: someone who finds evidence of a past they didn't know existed
archive_snapshot.desc: The Wayback Machine at archive.org allows public URL submission via its Save Page Now feature.

silent_follower.invite: someone whose movements have been quietly tracked
silent_follower.desc: Build or age a social media account on the platform the participant is most active on — Instagram or Twitter/X for most participants, LinkedIn for professionally-oriented arcs.

minor_wikipedia_edit.invite: someone whose world contains hidden layers of meaning they did not put there
minor_wikipedia_edit.desc: Identify a real Wikipedia article in the long tail — not a stub, but not a major article either — that connects to the participant's actual life through geography, profession, inte

maps_review_plant.invite: someone whose neighborhood knows something about them, or knows something they should know
maps_review_plant.desc: Write a Google Maps review for a location the participant frequents, posted from a Google account that belongs to or will belong to an arc character.

street_mark.invite: someone in whose neighborhood something has been quietly present
street_mark.desc: A human operator or confederate with local presence places a small, durable mark in the participant's regular environment.

book_bench_drop.invite: someone who finds things that seem meant for them
book_bench_drop.desc: Select a real book whose content is either thematically adjacent to the arc or contains a specific passage the arc will later quote.

physical_trace_plant.invite: someone in whose home something significant has been quietly sitting
physical_trace_plant.desc: A confederate with natural access to the participant's home or regular workspace places a small, innocuous object that will later be designated significant by the arc.

postcard_blind_send.invite: someone someone else is thinking of
postcard_blind_send.desc: Purchase or commission a postcard with a real photograph — a city street, a landscape, an architectural detail — from or associated with a city that the arc will later make signifi

classified_ad_plant.invite: someone who notices what others scroll past
classified_ad_plant.desc: Place a small classified advertisement in a local print publication — an alt-weekly, a neighborhood newspaper, a community bulletin.

character_bridge_connection.invite: someone who is one degree from something they did not know existed
character_bridge_connection.desc: Build or age a LinkedIn account for an arc character — a researcher, a consultant, a former colleague, a professional in a field adjacent to the arc's themes.

sighting_report.invite: someone who is being looked for
sighting_report.desc: Brief a confederate — someone in the participant's social circle who is willing to participate in a vague, socially plausible scenario — to deliver a single, casual observation in

ambient_triple_seed.invite: someone whose world has been quietly speaking a language they have not yet learned to read
ambient_triple_seed.desc: Select a single element — an image, a color pairing, a word, a number, a brief phrase — that will be the arc's central recurring identifier.

subscription_infiltration.invite: someone who receives things meant for them without knowing who sent them
subscription_infiltration.desc: Build or acquire a real, functional email newsletter — a Substack, a ConvertKit newsletter, a Mailchimp publication — on a topic adjacent to the arc's themes but broadly interestin

false_memory_seed.invite: someone who knew something before they knew they knew it
false_memory_seed.desc: Identify the arc's central informational premise — the claim, story, or concept that the arc will introduce as its revelatory content.

birthday_card_unknown_sender.invite: someone who is known to, and thought of by, a stranger
birthday_card_unknown_sender.desc: Mail a standard greeting card — a birthday card for a birthday, a seasonal card for a proximate holiday — to the participant at their mailing address.

glassdoor_reference_plant.invite: someone whose professional past contains a layer they did not know was there
glassdoor_reference_plant.desc: Create a Glassdoor account for the arc character and write a review of a company the participant has worked for or currently works at.

recurring_audio_seed.invite: someone whose world has developed a specific emotional tone before they understood why
recurring_audio_seed.desc: Select a specific song — one with emotional resonance appropriate to the arc's register, and one with low enough daily-life saturation that its recurrence over two weeks will feel

change_talk_elicitation.invite: the person who knows what this means for them; the one who can articulate their own story
change_talk_elicitation.desc: A structured conversation designed entirely around asking questions that cause the participant to articulate the significance of their experience themselves, rather than being told

outsider_witness_ceremony.invite: the person whose specific action was witnessed by someone who saw it clearly
outsider_witness_ceremony.desc: A structured moment where a character, confederate, or document witnesses a specific thing the participant did and names it back with precision.

generalization_contract.invite: the person who takes this somewhere; the one for whom this wasn't just an experience but a change
generalization_contract.desc: An explicit named commitment made by the participant at arc close about what they will apply from the experience to their real life.

front_loading_beat.invite: the participant who is about to learn something they don't know they need
front_loading_beat.desc: A beat deployed before the transformative core of the arc that names, in the arc's language, the insight or capacity the experience is designed to produce — without naming how or w

commissioning_letter.invite: the person who was commissioned; the one who has been equipped for something specific and sent forward to use it
commissioning_letter.desc: A commissioning letter is not a thank-you note, a certificate of completion, or a summary of the arc. It is a formal charge.

kinesthetic_encoding_beat.invite: the person who made something real with their body; whose transformation has physical evidence
kinesthetic_encoding_beat.desc: Physical acts produce memories with different durability and somatic integration than what is seen or heard.

stripping_ceremony.invite: the participant stripped of ordinary role markers; the person who has entered a new frame
stripping_ceremony.desc: A beat that marks the beginning of the liminal phase through removal, exchange, or surrender of something that belongs to the participant's ordinary identity.

failure_cascade_reframe.invite: the person who wasn't wrong to be skeptical; the one whose doubt was the most interesting response possible
failure_cascade_reframe.desc: Deployed when a participant has gone quiet, stopped responding, or signaled skepticism — not as a counter-move but as an acknowledgment.

knowledge_frontier_seed.invite: the one who knows they don't know something important; the investigator who has identified a gap
knowledge_frontier_seed.desc: Source: Outer Wilds (Mobius Digital, 2019) as the canonical model for knowledge-frontier design. The expanding edge of what you don't know yet is itself a reward.

player_journal_artifact.invite: the archivist of their own story; the witness to their own transformation; the author, not the character
player_journal_artifact.desc: Source: EVE Online player journalism model — the players who documented their experience of EVE (blogs, player news organizations, after-action reports) created artifacts of signif

temporal_rupture_opening.invite: the participant who has stepped out of ordinary time; the one inside a different frame
temporal_rupture_opening.desc: An explicit declaration — delivered through the arc's channel — that ordinary time has suspended and a different kind of time has begun.

ordeal_threshold.invite: the one who went through it; the one who proved something to themselves by not stopping
ordeal_threshold.desc: A calibrated physical challenge that creates genuine sunk cost and genuine self-knowledge — not theatrical difficulty but actual discomfort that requires the participant to decide

absent_witness_invocation.invite: the person who has sworn before something larger than the present room; the one whose commitment was witnessed by an authority no one needs to verify
absent_witness_invocation.desc: The participant makes a commitment before an absent authority — the organization, the dead, the ideal future self, a principle, a figure who cannot be present.

communitas_beat.invite: the participant who is neither above nor below the guide — the equal, briefly; the person who has access to this specific contact
communitas_beat.desc: Victor Turner's communitas: the temporary dissolution of hierarchy in the liminal phase, producing a direct, equal social bond between participants who are ordinarily differently-p

layered_secret_system.invite: the participant who has been inducted into one level and knows there are deeper ones; the initiate who is not yet fully in
layered_secret_system.desc: A revelation structure with multiple degrees of disclosure, each of which feels conclusive until the next is accessible.

benefactor_capture.invite: before reveal: the competent operative, the trusted agent · after reveal: the one who must decide what to do with what they now know
benefactor_capture.desc: The structural pattern for the deepest trust betrayal in an immersive arc: the participant's guide/handler has been the source of information, missions, and support throughout; the

manufactured_crisis_reveal.invite: the one who now knows the full picture; the one who must decide what to do with that knowledge
manufactured_crisis_reveal.desc: The Deus Ex Gray Death / Ambrosia structure: participant has been operating within a crisis frame — believed the threat was real, responded in good faith.

fractal_revelation.invite: the one who keeps going even when the bottom keeps moving; the investigator who has accepted there is no final layer — until there is
fractal_revelation.desc: A beat that resolves one layer of mystery while simultaneously opening at least two new questions at a deeper level.

distributed_truth_fragment.invite: the investigator who triangulates; the one who reconstructs truth from fragments rather than receiving it
distributed_truth_fragment.desc: The datacube model from Deus Ex: a single evidence fragment planted in a context the participant accesses for non-arc reasons, which only becomes fully meaningful when cross-refere

strategic_silence_beat.invite: the one who trusted the partial picture; retroactively: the one who completed the false inference themselves
strategic_silence_beat.desc: The JC/Manderley mechanic: the character gives a technically accurate answer that is actively misleading — not because they assert something false, but because they permit the part

temporal_loop_architecture.invite: the one who has been here before; the participant who has mapped the system while the naive participant is still discovering it; the strategist, not the tourist
temporal_loop_architecture.desc: The loop is a knowledge architecture, not a content architecture.

anomaly_attendance.invite: the one who showed up; the participant whose physical presence was real and counted
anomaly_attendance.desc: The Niantic Anomaly mechanic: a faction event requires physical attendance at a specific real-world location during a specific window. Body presence is logged.

territorial_claim.invite: the one who holds this ground; the keeper of a specific real place
territorial_claim.desc: From Ingress portal ownership: a specific real-world location is designated as a game node. Participant deploys resources to own it; ownership is recorded; others can challenge.

queryable_legacy.invite: the participant with a history; the one whose contributions are verifiable and permanent
queryable_legacy.desc: The queryable ledger: participant actions are logged in a persistent, externally accessible database.

trace_exposure.invite: the one being traced; the operative whose security was imperfect; the person who left a trail they didn't know existed
trace_exposure.desc: From Uplink's link monitor mechanic: while performing a digital task, participant receives real-time signal that their connection is being traced — a progress indicator, a characte

contract_complicity.invite: the effective operative who didn't know what the mission was for; the one who did the job well before learning what the job was
contract_complicity.desc: From Uplink's contract structure: participant receives a formal job offer. Terms: perform tasks X, Y, Z. Payment: information or resource. The participant accepts.

active_erasure.invite: the one who chose to cover their tracks; the operative who destroyed the evidence; the participant who made an active choice to conceal
active_erasure.desc: From Uplink's cover-your-tracks mechanic: participant must actively delete logs, destroy evidence, or clean a trail.

forum_intervention.invite: the one who intervened; the protagonist who stopped the scene and changed it; the person who knows what they'd do now
forum_intervention.desc: From Boal's Forum Theater adapted for single participant: a scene is reconstructed — a moment of constraint or failed agency that the participant knows from their own life or from

image_theater_freeze.invite: the observer who can read power through bodies; the person whose own physical stance reveals their relational position
image_theater_freeze.desc: From Boal's Image Theater: participant is asked to pose their body to represent a situation, feeling, or power dynamic — without words.

invisible_theater_event.invite: the one who acted (or didn't) when they thought it was real; the participant who can look back at their spontaneous response as data about themselves
invisible_theater_event.desc: From Boal's Invisible Theater: a scene plays out in the participant's real environment without their knowing it was designed. The scene appears real.

mask_anonymity_passage.invite: the anonymous explorer; the self behind the social face; the one who can ask questions they'd never ask as themselves
mask_anonymity_passage.desc: From Felix Barrett / Punchdrunk mask mechanic: participant is given a mask before entering the experience space.

one_on_one_private_scene.invite: the one who was chosen; the participant whose specific presence matters; not an audience member but a protagonist
one_on_one_private_scene.desc: From Punchdrunk's one-on-one mechanic: a performer selects a single audience member, takes them into a private room, closes the door, and performs a 2-3 minute scene — confession,

environmental_narrative_space.invite: the investigator who reads space; the participant who knows how to look; the archaeologist of other lives
environmental_narrative_space.desc: From Barrett's site-sympathetic approach: a physical space is pre-loaded with objects that carry narrative information but do not explain themselves.

hell_replay.invite: the participant who carries this moment; the one for whom this was the decisive thing
hell_replay.desc: From Jeepform's "hell" technique: a scene that was played at full realism is replayed in compressed, accelerated, or stylized form.

framekick_temporal_shift.invite: the participant who holds multiple timelines; the one who understands that the past moment and the present moment are connected in a way not yet legible
framekick_temporal_shift.desc: From Jeepform's framekick: mid-scene, without warning, the frame shifts.

internal_monologue_voiced.invite: the participant who can be both actor and observer of themselves; the self who speaks their experience rather than performing it
internal_monologue_voiced.desc: From Jeepform meta-technique: in a live scene, a participant pauses their character's action and speaks their character's internal state directly, in first person, as if the inner

author_narration_beat.invite: the protagonist of a story being told by an author who sees more than they do; the participant who discovers their experience has already been witnessed and named
author_narration_beat.desc: From Jeepform's narrative voice-over / author stance: a narrating voice distinct from any character in the fiction speaks the participant's experience from outside.

group_synchronization.invite: the one who thought they were working alone; now part of something larger than themselves
group_synchronization.desc: The first moment previously-separate participants discover each other.

faction_formation.invite: depends on faction role — the accelerant, the anchor, the escalator, the skeptic; each participant's drivermap profile predicts their faction
faction_formation.desc: Ambient ramp — no operator action required. After group_synchronization, participants are in contact with each other and have different profiles, different framings of the evidence

shared_crisis_beat.invite: one of us; the person who held their post when the ship went down; the one who was there
shared_crisis_beat.desc: A simultaneous world-state event — ship under attack, building compromised, operation blown — that all participants experience at the same moment, requiring collective real-time response.

faction_allegiance_fork.invite: someone who chose; the one who knew which side they were on; the person whose decision defined the arc's second half
faction_allegiance_fork.desc: An explicit, diegetically consequential allegiance declaration that permanently routes the participant into one of two exclusive story tracks.

app_location_dispatch.invite: the agent dispatched into the field; the operative who reads their phone and goes
app_location_dispatch.desc: A mobile interface dispatches location-specific missions requiring physical navigation — receive dispatch, navigate to location, interact with cast or object, unlock next dispatch.

nested_permission_cascade.invite: someone who chose this, step by step; the participant who discovers, at the arc's threshold, that they have already arrived
nested_permission_cascade.desc: A deliberately designed sequence of small, low-stakes consent moments that aggregate into a major identity shift — each micro-yes diegetically coherent in isolation.

counter_identity_encounter.invite: someone looking at a mirror with distance; the participant who meets who they might have been, or who they are becoming
counter_identity_encounter.desc: An arranged meeting with a confederate who embodies a radically different possible self — someone the participant could have become, is at risk of becoming, or once feared becoming.

reintegration_dispatch.invite: someone who is still in process; the participant who discovers the arc continues to hold them after they have left
reintegration_dispatch.desc: A timed sequence of post-arc contacts dispatched at 3 days, 3 weeks, and 3 months after arc conclusion, scaffolding identity shift against re-socialization pressure.
